  select(filename, wavenumber, absorbance, baseline_corrected, snv_plusOne) %>%
  group_by(filename) %>%
  summarise(
    n_points = n(),
    min_norm = min(snv_plusOne, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_norm = max(snv_plusOne, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_norm = mean(snv_plusOne, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  head()
## # A tibble: 6 × 5
##   filename                                  n_points min_norm max_norm mean_norm
##   <chr>                                        <int>    <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>
## 1 DRIFTS_Pot001_13Cwheat_wk0_rhizoSoil_260…        8    0.984    1.02      1.01 
## 2 DRIFTS_Pot012_13Csoy_wk10_rhizoSoil_2606…        8    0.772    0.830     0.804
## 3 DRIFTS_Pot020_12CnoPlant_wk30_rhizoSoil_…        8    0.972    1.02      0.997
## 4 DRIFTS_Pot020_12CnoPlant_wk40_rhizoSoil_…        8    0.899    0.958     0.931
## 5 DRIFTS_Pot024_13Cwheat_wk0_rhizoSoil_260…        8    0.780    0.829     0.806
## 6 DRIFTS_Pot028_13Cwheat_wk0_rhizoSoil_260…        8    0.828    0.879     0.857

Compare soil & roots

Import root data and join with soil data

This chunk imports the integrated root data and checks that each pot ID and timepoint combination in the root data has a corresponding entry in the soil data. It then creates a combined dataset including only the crop and timepoint combinations that exist in both datasets, summarizes the data, and counts the number of samples for each crop and timepoint in the combined dataset.

# import root data
root_data_file <- file.path(box_base, "Salk Institute Project/AKS Salk files/Adrianne_FTIRdata/integrated_analysis_outputs/processed_int_data_roots.csv") # WILL NEED TO CHANGE THIS

root_data <- read_csv(root_data_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Check that each ID and timepoint combination in the root data has a corresponding entry in the soil data
missing_combinations <- anti_join(root_data, ftir_data, by = c("ID", "timepoint"))
if (nrow(missing_combinations) > 0) {
  warning("The following pot ID and timepoint combinations are present in the root data but missing in the soil data:\n",
          paste(apply(missing_combinations, 1, function(row) paste(row["ID"], row["timepoint"], sep = " - ")), collapse = "\n"))
} else {
  cat("All pot ID and timepoint combinations in the root data have corresponding entries in the soil data.\n")
}

# Create a combined dataset including only the crop and timepoint combinations that exist in both datasets
combined_data <- inner_join(ftir_data, root_data, by = c("ID", "timepoint", "crop"), suffix = c("_soil", "_root")) %>%
  select(-starts_with("type_"), -starts_with("int_"), -starts_with("total_peak_area"), -starts_with("aliphatic_")) %>% # remove unnecessary columns
  mutate(crop_timepoint = paste(crop, timepoint, sep = "_")) # combined crop x timepoint grouping -- matches the keys used in color_map (theme_colors_config.R)
str(combined_data) # check the structure of the combined dataset
## tibble [27 × 22] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ sample_soil                    : chr [1:27] "DRIFTS_Pot001_13Cwheat_wk0_rhizoSoil_260618.0.dpt" "DRIFTS_Pot012_13Csoy_wk10_rhizoSoil_260624.0.dpt" "DRIFTS_Pot029_13Cwheat_wk10_rhizoSoil_260624.0.dpt" "DRIFTS_Pot029_13Cwheat_wk40_rhizoSoil_260702.0.dpt" ...
##  $ crop                           : chr [1:27] "wheat" "soy" "wheat" "wheat" ...
##  $ timepoint                      : chr [1:27] "wk0" "wk10" "wk10" "wk40" ...
##  $ ID                             : chr [1:27] "001" "012" "029" "029" ...
##  $ simple_plant_prop_soil         : num [1:27] 0.354 0.282 0.306 0.343 0.32 ...
##  $ complex_plant_prop_soil        : num [1:27] 0.167 0.217 0.199 0.183 0.203 ...
##  $ microbial_prop_soil            : num [1:27] 0.479 0.501 0.495 0.473 0.477 ...
##  $ simple_microbial_prop_soil     : num [1:27] 0.739 0.564 0.617 0.725 0.671 ...
##  $ complex_microbial_prop_soil    : num [1:27] 0.35 0.433 0.401 0.387 0.426 ...
##  $ total_plant_prop_soil          : num [1:27] 0.521 0.499 0.505 0.527 0.523 ...
##  $ total_plant_microbial_prop_soil: num [1:27] 1.089 0.997 1.018 1.112 1.097 ...
##  $ simple_complex_prop_soil       : num [1:27] 2.11 1.3 1.54 1.88 1.57 ...
##  $ sample_root                    : chr [1:27] "DRIFTS_pot001_13Cwheat_wk0_root_250725.0.dpt" "DRIFTS_pot012_13Csoy_wk10_root_250827.0.dpt" "DRIFTS_pot029_13Cwheat_wk10_root_250827.0.dpt" "DRIFTS_pot029_13Cwheat_wk40_root_250827.3.dpt" ...
##  $ simple_plant_prop_root         : num [1:27] 0.431 0.392 0.427 0.421 0.389 ...
##  $ complex_plant_prop_root        : num [1:27] 0.186 0.191 0.186 0.206 0.209 ...
##  $ microbial_prop_root            : num [1:27] 0.383 0.417 0.387 0.373 0.402 ...
##  $ simple_microbial_prop_root     : num [1:27] 1.125 0.941 1.102 1.13 0.969 ...
##  $ complex_microbial_prop_root    : num [1:27] 0.484 0.457 0.48 0.554 0.522 ...
##  $ total_plant_prop_root          : num [1:27] 0.617 0.583 0.613 0.627 0.598 ...
##  $ total_plant_microbial_prop_root: num [1:27] 1.61 1.4 1.58 1.68 1.49 ...
##  $ simple_complex_prop_root       : num [1:27] 2.32 2.06 2.3 2.04 1.86 ...
##  $ crop_timepoint                 : chr [1:27] "wheat_wk0" "soy_wk10" "wheat_wk10" "wheat_wk40" ...
summary(combined_data) # check the summary statistics of the combined dataset
##  sample_soil            crop            timepoint              ID           
##  Length:27          Length:27          Length:27          Length:27         
##  Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character  
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##                                                                             
##  simple_plant_prop_soil complex_plant_prop_soil microbial_prop_soil
##  Min.   :0.2772         Min.   :0.1675          Min.   :0.4630     
##  1st Qu.:0.3125         1st Qu.:0.1825          1st Qu.:0.4742     
##  Median :0.3239         Median :0.1927          Median :0.4834     
##  Mean   :0.3254         Mean   :0.1922          Mean   :0.4825     
##  3rd Qu.:0.3431         3rd Qu.:0.2000          3rd Qu.:0.4917     
##  Max.   :0.3580         Max.   :0.2219          Max.   :0.5009     
##  simple_microbial_prop_soil complex_microbial_prop_soil total_plant_prop_soil
##  Min.   :0.5535             Min.   :0.3498              Min.   :0.4991       
##  1st Qu.:0.6393             1st Qu.:0.3859              1st Qu.:0.5083       
##  Median :0.6708             Median :0.4017              Median :0.5166       
##  Mean   :0.6758             Mean   :0.3982              Mean   :0.5175       
##  3rd Qu.:0.7256             3rd Qu.:0.4100              3rd Qu.:0.5258       
##  Max.   :0.7722             Max.   :0.4430              Max.   :0.5370       
##  total_plant_microbial_prop_soil simple_complex_prop_soil sample_root       
##  Min.   :0.9965                  Min.   :1.249            Length:27         
##  1st Qu.:1.0336                  1st Qu.:1.565            Class :character  
##  Median :1.0685                  Median :1.677            Mode  :character  
##  Mean   :1.0740                  Mean   :1.708                              
##  3rd Qu.:1.1088                  3rd Qu.:1.889                              
##  Max.   :1.1598                  Max.   :2.122                              
##  simple_plant_prop_root complex_plant_prop_root microbial_prop_root
##  Min.   :0.3626         Min.   :0.1661          Min.   :0.3498     
##  1st Qu.:0.4012         1st Qu.:0.1842          1st Qu.:0.3706     
##  Median :0.4233         Median :0.1907          Median :0.3840     
##  Mean   :0.4216         Mean   :0.1927          Mean   :0.3857     
##  3rd Qu.:0.4378         3rd Qu.:0.2029          3rd Qu.:0.3971     
##  Max.   :0.4841         Max.   :0.2163          Max.   :0.4297     
##  simple_microbial_prop_root complex_microbial_prop_root total_plant_prop_root
##  Min.   :0.861              Min.   :0.4380              Min.   :0.5703       
##  1st Qu.:1.008              1st Qu.:0.4808              1st Qu.:0.6029       
##  Median :1.102              Median :0.4954              Median :0.6160       
##  Mean   :1.100              Mean   :0.5002              Mean   :0.6143       
##  3rd Qu.:1.177              3rd Qu.:0.5219              3rd Qu.:0.6294       
##  Max.   :1.384              Max.   :0.5540              Max.   :0.6502       
##  total_plant_microbial_prop_root simple_complex_prop_root crop_timepoint    
##  Min.   :1.327                   Min.   :1.676            Length:27         
##  1st Qu.:1.518                   1st Qu.:1.997            Class :character  
##  Median :1.604                   Median :2.234            Mode  :character  
##  Mean   :1.600                   Mean   :2.203                              
##  3rd Qu.:1.698                   3rd Qu.:2.353                              
##  Max.   :1.859                   Max.   :2.916
# save to CSV for reference
write_csv(combined_data, file.path(outputs_folder, "combined_soil_root_data.csv"))
# Count the number of samples for each crop and timepoint in the combined dataset and list the IDs for each group
sample_counts <- combined_data %>%
  group_by(crop, timepoint) %>%
  summarise(n_samples = n(), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  # add list of IDs for each crop and timepoint combination
  left_join(
    combined_data %>%
      group_by(crop, timepoint) %>%
      summarise(sample_IDs = paste(unique(ID), collapse = ", "), .groups = 'drop'),
    by = c("crop", "timepoint")
  )
print(sample_counts) # check the sample counts for each crop and timepoint
## # A tibble: 11 × 4
##    crop    timepoint n_samples sample_IDs   
##    <chr>   <chr>         <int> <chr>        
##  1 noPlant wk30              1 052          
##  2 noPlant wk40              1 052          
##  3 rice    wk0               1 103          
##  4 rice    wk10              5 055, 087, 119
##  5 rice    wk40              3 055, 087, 119
##  6 soy     wk0               2 050, 062     
##  7 soy     wk10              4 012, 080, 094
##  8 soy     wk40              3 030, 080, 094
##  9 wheat   wk0               2 001, 032     
## 10 wheat   wk10              2 029, 053     
## 11 wheat   wk40              3 029, 053, 109

Create function to generate scatterplots comparing soil and root proportions

This chunk defines a function that takes “region” as it’s only variable, and generates a scatterplot comparing the soil and root proportions for that region. The function is then called for each of the three regions: simple plant, complex plant, and microbial.

# create a function to generate scatterplots comparing soil and root proportions for a given region
# “region” variable is a proportion to compare (“simple_plant”, “complex_plant”, or “microbial”)
#Function plots all ID-timepoint combinations as points on a scatterplot: 
## the x-axis is the root proportion
## the y-axis is the soil proportion 
## Points are colored by crop & timepoint as defined by the color_map variable config\theme_colors_config.R 

soil_v_root_plot <- function(region, crops_ordered = c("soy", "rice", "wheat")) {
  # Define the column names for soil and root proportions based on the region
  soil_col <- paste0(region, "_prop_soil")
  root_col <- paste0(region, "_prop_root")
  region_label <- str_to_title(gsub("_", " ", region)) # Convert to title case for labeling
    # find the minimum and maximum values for the whole dataset to set the limits of the plot (same for both axes)
  min_val <- min(combined_data[[root_col]], combined_data[[soil_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  max_val <- max(combined_data[[root_col]], combined_data[[soil_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  range <- max_val - min_val
  # Assign each point's color directly from its crop's own sub-palette (via crop_timepoint ->
  # color_map lookup), so the real data layer doesn't carry a mapped color aesthetic. This lets
  # us build one legend block per crop (via dummy layers + ggnewscale) the same way
  # build_stacked_range_plot() does, instead of one flat crop_timepoint legend.
  plot_data <- combined_data %>%
    filter(crop != "noPlant") %>%
    mutate(point_color = unname(color_map[as.character(crop_timepoint)])) %>%
    as.data.frame()
  
  # Create a scatterplot comparing soil and root proportions for the specified region
  # plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes_string(x = root_col, y = soil_col, color = "crop_timepoint")) +
  #   geom_point(aes(color = I(point_color)), size = 3, alpha = 0.98) +
  plot <- ggplot() +
    geom_point(data = plot_data,
               mapping = aes_string(x = root_col, y = soil_col, color = "point_color"),
               size = 3, alpha = 0.98) +
    scale_color_identity() +

    xlim(min_val - 0.05 * range, max_val + 0.05 * range) +
    ylim(min_val - 0.05 * range, max_val + 0.05 * range) +
    labs(title = paste("Soil vs Root ", region_label, "OM Proportion"),
         x = "Proportion of Root OM",
         y = "Proportion of Soil OM"
         ) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.direction = "vertical", # lays out timepoint labels within each crop's legend block
      legend.box = "vertical", # lays out the legend blocks 
      legend.spacing.y = unit(0.1, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 11.5, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(17, 17, 7, 17)
    ) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = annotation_line_color) # Add a reference line (y=x)
 # Build one legend block per crop using invisible dummy layers + ggnewscale, so each
  # crop keeps its own color palette in its own titled legend block (same approach as
  # build_stacked_range_plot()).
  if (!require(ggnewscale, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("ggnewscale")
    library(ggnewscale)
  }
  for (i in seq_along(crops_ordered)) {
    crop_name <- crops_ordered[i]
    # Only include timepoints that actually have data for this crop
    present_tps <- names(color_map_all_sep[[crop_name]])[
      names(color_map_all_sep[[crop_name]]) %in% unique(as.character(plot_data$timepoint[plot_data$crop == crop_name]))
    ]
    if (length(present_tps) == 0) next

    dummy_data <- data.frame(
      x = rep(min_val, length(present_tps)),
      y = rep(min_val, length(present_tps)),
      tp = present_tps
    )
    plot <- plot +
      ggnewscale::new_scale_color() +
      geom_point(data = dummy_data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = tp), size = 0, alpha = 0) +
      scale_color_manual(
        name = str_to_title(crop_name),
        values = color_map_all_sep[[crop_name]][present_tps],
        breaks = present_tps,
        labels = present_tps,
        guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4, alpha = 1), order = i)
      )
   }
  return(plot)
}
soil_v_root_simple_plot <- soil_v_root_plot("simple_plant")
soil_v_root_complex_plot <- soil_v_root_plot("complex_plant")
soil_v_root_microbial_plot <- soil_v_root_plot("microbial")

print(soil_v_root_simple_plot)

print(soil_v_root_complex_plot)

print(soil_v_root_microbial_plot)

Root vs. soil regression statistics

This chunk fits a linear regression of soil proportion on root proportion (soil ~ root) for each functional-group region (simple plant, complex plant, microbial), and reports R², slope, and p-value (i) across all data points, (ii) grouped by timepoint, and (iii) grouped by crop.

# Human-readable labels for each functional group region
region_labels <- c(simple_plant = "Simple Plant", complex_plant = "Complex Plant", microbial = "Microbial")

# Helper: fit soil_prop ~ root_prop for a given region, optionally within groups of a
# grouping variable (e.g. "timepoint" or "crop"). Returns R-squared, slope, and p-value.
calc_soil_root_regression <- function(data, region, group_var = NULL) {
  root_col <- paste0(region, "_prop_root")
  soil_col <- paste0(region, "_prop_soil")

  fit_one <- function(df, group_label) {
    df <- df[stats::complete.cases(df[, c(root_col, soil_col)]), ]
    # Need at least 3 points and some spread in x to fit a meaningful regression
    if (nrow(df) < 3 || stats::sd(df[[root_col]]) == 0) {
      return(data.frame(group = group_label, n = nrow(df),
                         r_squared = NA_real_, slope = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_))
    }
    model <- lm(reformulate(root_col, response = soil_col), data = df)
    model_summary <- summary(model)
    data.frame(
      group = group_label,
      n = nrow(df),
      r_squared = model_summary$r.squared,
      slope = unname(coef(model)[2]),
      p_value = model_summary$coefficients[2, 4]
    )
  }

  if (is.null(group_var)) {
    return(fit_one(data, "All data"))
  }

  groups <- unique(data[[group_var]])
  # Order groups sensibly: timepoints numerically, crops in a fixed order, everything else alphabetically
  if (group_var == "timepoint") {
    groups <- groups[order(as.numeric(str_extract(groups, "\\d+")))]
  } else if (group_var == "crop") {
    crop_order <- c("wheat", "rice", "soy", "noPlant")
    groups <- groups[order(match(groups, crop_order))]
  } else {
    groups <- sort(groups)
  }

  do.call(rbind, lapply(groups, function(g) fit_one(data[data[[group_var]] == g, ], as.character(g))))
}

# Calculate stats for each region: (i) all data, (ii) by timepoint, (iii) by crop
soil_root_regression_stats <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(region_labels), function(region) {
  stats_region <- rbind(
    calc_soil_root_regression(combined_data, region, group_var = NULL),
    calc_soil_root_regression(combined_data, region, group_var = "timepoint"),
    calc_soil_root_regression(combined_data, region, group_var = "crop")
  )
  stats_region$region <- region_labels[[region]]
  stats_region
}))

# Tidy up for display
soil_root_regression_stats <- soil_root_regression_stats %>%
  select(region, group, n, r_squared, slope, p_value) %>%
  mutate(
    r_squared = round(r_squared, 3),
    slope = round(slope, 3),
    p_value = case_when(
      is.na(p_value) ~ NA_character_,
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "< 0.001",
      TRUE ~ format(round(p_value, 3), nsmall = 3)
    )
  )

knitr::kable(soil_root_regression_stats,
             col.names = c("Region", "Group", "n", "R\u00b2", "Slope", "p-value"),
             row.names = FALSE)
Region Group n R² Slope p-value
Simple Plant All data 27 0.029 -0.133 0.400
Simple Plant wk0 5 0.143 0.186 0.531
Simple Plant wk10 11 0.005 -0.072 0.829
Simple Plant wk30 1 NA NA NA
Simple Plant wk40 10 0.005 -0.055 0.839
Simple Plant wheat 7 0.308 -0.459 0.196
Simple Plant rice 9 0.001 0.027 0.935
Simple Plant soy 9 0.003 -0.051 0.891
Simple Plant noPlant 2 NA NA NA
Complex Plant All data 27 0.008 -0.099 0.666
Complex Plant wk0 5 0.202 0.684 0.448
Complex Plant wk10 11 0.058 -0.658 0.475
Complex Plant wk30 1 NA NA NA
Complex Plant wk40 10 0.015 0.169 0.739
Complex Plant wheat 7 0.137 -0.302 0.413
Complex Plant rice 9 0.134 0.511 0.332
Complex Plant soy 9 0.024 0.164 0.692
Complex Plant noPlant 2 NA NA NA
Microbial All data 27 0.011 -0.061 0.605
Microbial wk0 5 0.033 0.026 0.770
Microbial wk10 11 0.004 -0.024 0.853
Microbial wk30 1 NA NA NA
Microbial wk40 10 0.138 -0.197 0.291
Microbial wheat 7 0.160 -0.329 0.373
Microbial rice 9 0.001 -0.024 0.925
Microbial soy 9 0.002 -0.032 0.913
Microbial noPlant 2 NA NA NA

Root vs. soil: percent change (wk10 -> wk40, within-pot)

Since pot IDs are tracked longitudinally starting at wk10 (wk0 pots were destroyed and not resampled), this section calculates a within-pot percent change from wk10 to wk40, instead of comparing against a crop-level baseline. Each pot’s own wk10 measurement is the denominator, for both root and soil, so the two are directly comparable and the pairing is exact (same physical pot at both timepoints).

Calculate within-pot percent change (wk10 -> wk40)

regions <- c("simple_plant", "complex_plant", "microbial")
root_cols <- paste0(regions, "_prop_root")
soil_cols <- paste0(regions, "_prop_soil")

wk10_vals <- combined_data %>%
  filter(timepoint == "wk10") %>%
  filter(!sample_soil %in% c("DRIFTS_Pot055_13Crice_wk10_rhizoSoil_260624A.0.dpt", "DRIFTS_Pot119_13Crice_wk10_rhizoSoil_260624B.0.dpt")) %>% # filter out rice duplicates
  select(crop, ID, all_of(c(root_cols, soil_cols))) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_wk10"), all_of(c(root_cols, soil_cols)))

wk40_vals <- combined_data %>%
  filter(timepoint == "wk40") %>%
  select(crop, ID, all_of(c(root_cols, soil_cols))) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_wk40"), all_of(c(root_cols, soil_cols)))

# Check that each pot ID present at one timepoint is also present at the other
missing_wk40 <- anti_join(wk10_vals, wk40_vals, by = c("crop", "ID"))
missing_wk10 <- anti_join(wk40_vals, wk10_vals, by = c("crop", "ID"))

if (nrow(missing_wk40) > 0) {
  cat("Pot IDs with wk10 data but no matching wk40 data (excluded from within-pot comparison):\n")
  print(as.data.frame(missing_wk40 %>% select(crop, ID)))
}
## Pot IDs with wk10 data but no matching wk40 data (excluded from within-pot comparison):
##   crop  ID
## 1  soy 012
if (nrow(missing_wk10) > 0) {
  cat("Pot IDs with wk40 data but no matching wk10 data (excluded from within-pot comparison):\n")
  print(as.data.frame(missing_wk10 %>% select(crop, ID)))
}
## Pot IDs with wk40 data but no matching wk10 data (excluded from within-pot comparison):
##      crop  ID
## 1     soy 030
## 2 noPlant 052
## 3   wheat 109
# Pair each pot ID's wk10 and wk40 measurements -- true within-pot comparison
change_data_wk10_wk40 <- inner_join(wk10_vals, wk40_vals, by = c("crop", "ID"))

for (region in regions) {
  root_wk10 <- paste0(region, "_prop_root_wk10")
  root_wk40 <- paste0(region, "_prop_root_wk40")
  soil_wk10 <- paste0(region, "_prop_soil_wk10")
  soil_wk40 <- paste0(region, "_prop_soil_wk40")

  change_data_wk10_wk40[[paste0(region, "_root_pct_change")]] <-
    (change_data_wk10_wk40[[root_wk40]] - change_data_wk10_wk40[[root_wk10]]) / change_data_wk10_wk40[[root_wk10]] * 100
  change_data_wk10_wk40[[paste0(region, "_soil_pct_change")]] <-
    (change_data_wk10_wk40[[soil_wk40]] - change_data_wk10_wk40[[soil_wk10]]) / change_data_wk10_wk40[[soil_wk10]] * 100
}

change_data_wk10_wk40 <- change_data_wk10_wk40 %>%
  select(crop, ID, ends_with("_pct_change"))

cat("\nWithin-pot wk10 -> wk40 change dataset:", nrow(change_data_wk10_wk40), "pots\n")
## 
## Within-pot wk10 -> wk40 change dataset: 8 pots
str(change_data_wk10_wk40)
## tibble [8 × 8] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ crop                         : chr [1:8] "wheat" "wheat" "rice" "soy" ...
##  $ ID                           : chr [1:8] "029" "053" "055" "080" ...
##  $ simple_plant_root_pct_change : num [1:8] -1.31 -10.64 3.39 -9.49 2.11 ...
##  $ simple_plant_soil_pct_change : num [1:8] 12.294 18.279 -0.138 9.351 -1.333 ...
##  $ complex_plant_root_pct_change: num [1:8] 11 12.63 4.02 13.94 2.84 ...
##  $ complex_plant_soil_pct_change: num [1:8] -7.82 -10.17 1.86 -2.63 13.4 ...
##  $ microbial_root_pct_change    : num [1:8] -3.83 6.73 -5.65 4.25 -3.55 ...
##  $ microbial_soil_pct_change    : num [1:8] -4.45 -6.81 -0.66 -4.98 -3.91 ...
summary(change_data_wk10_wk40)
##      crop                ID            simple_plant_root_pct_change
##  Length:8           Length:8           Min.   :-10.6383            
##  Class :character   Class :character   1st Qu.:-10.2200            
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Median : -5.3999            
##                                        Mean   : -4.6540            
##                                        3rd Qu.: -0.1166            
##                                        Max.   :  3.3926            
##  simple_plant_soil_pct_change complex_plant_root_pct_change
##  Min.   :-1.333               Min.   : 2.007               
##  1st Qu.: 4.292               1st Qu.: 3.728               
##  Median : 9.911               Median :11.035               
##  Mean   : 8.739               Mean   : 8.571               
##  3rd Qu.:13.026               3rd Qu.:11.456               
##  Max.   :18.279               Max.   :13.937               
##  complex_plant_soil_pct_change microbial_root_pct_change
##  Min.   :-10.167               Min.   :-5.650           
##  1st Qu.: -6.363               1st Qu.:-3.624           
##  Median : -2.403               Median : 2.129           
##  Mean   : -1.355               Mean   : 1.350           
##  3rd Qu.:  2.039               3rd Qu.: 6.428           
##  Max.   : 13.402               Max.   : 6.727           
##  microbial_soil_pct_change
##  Min.   :-6.8839          
##  1st Qu.:-6.0961          
##  Median :-4.8602          
##  Mean   :-4.7873          
##  3rd Qu.:-4.3181          
##  Max.   :-0.6601
# save as CSV for reference
write_csv(change_data_wk10_wk40, file.path(outputs_folder, "within_pot_change_data_wk10_wk40.csv"))

Create function to generate within-pot percent-change scatterplots

Plots showing the change in each OM-proportion as a percentage difference from wk10 to wk40. Each point is one pot’s own wk10 -> wk40 change (true pairing, not a crop average). Dashed reference line shows a slope of -1 (proportional relationship). Wheat and soy roots decrease in simple plant OM proportion, while the adjacent rhizosphere soil increases in simple plant OM proportion, suggesting that the decomposing roots are contributing simple aliphatics to the soil OM pool.

print_square_plot <- function(plot, panel_size = unit(5.8, "in")) {
  fixed_plot <- egg::set_panel_size(plot, width = panel_size, height = panel_size)
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(fixed_plot)
}
soil_v_root_change_plot_wk10_wk40 <- function(region) {
  root_col <- paste0(region, "_root_pct_change")
  soil_col <- paste0(region, "_soil_pct_change")
  # find the minimum and maximum values for the whole dataset to set the limits of the plot
  min_val <- min(change_data_wk10_wk40[[root_col]], change_data_wk10_wk40[[soil_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  max_val <- max(change_data_wk10_wk40[[root_col]], change_data_wk10_wk40[[soil_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  range <- max_val - min_val
  lower <- min_val - 0.05 * range
  upper <- max_val + 0.05 * range
  step <- max(round(0.15 * range, 0), 1)  # 15% of the rangeor some sensible minimum
  breaks <- seq(
  from = step * floor(lower / step),
  to   = step * ceiling(upper / step),
  by   = step
)


  plot <- ggplot(change_data_wk10_wk40, aes_string(x = root_col, y = soil_col, color = "crop")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = annotation_line_color) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = annotation_line_color) +
    geom_point(size = 3.3, alpha = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = crop_colors, labels = crop_labels) +
    # set the limits of the plot to be slightly larger than the min and max values to avoid cutting off points at the edges
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(lower, upper), breaks = breaks) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(lower, upper), breaks = breaks) +
    labs(title = paste("Change in", str_to_title(gsub("_", " ", region)), "OM Proportion"),
         x = "% Change in Roots",
         y = "% Change in Rhizosphere Soil") +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 11.5, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.margin = margin(l = 10, t = 7, r = 7, b = 7),
      aspect.ratio = 1  # Ensure equal scaling for x and y axes
    ) +
    geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = annotation_line_color) # Add a reference line (y=-x)
  ggsave(
    file.path(outputs_folder, sub("\\.png$", paste0(theme_suffix, ".png"),
    paste0(region, "_change_plot_wk10_wk40.png"))),
    plot
    )

  return(plot)
}

soil_v_root_change_wk10_wk40_simple_plot    <- soil_v_root_change_plot_wk10_wk40("simple_plant")
soil_v_root_change_wk10_wk40_complex_plot   <- soil_v_root_change_plot_wk10_wk40("complex_plant")
soil_v_root_change_wk10_wk40_microbial_plot <- soil_v_root_change_plot_wk10_wk40("microbial")

print_square_plot(soil_v_root_change_wk10_wk40_simple_plot)

print_square_plot(soil_v_root_change_wk10_wk40_complex_plot)

print_square_plot(soil_v_root_change_wk10_wk40_microbial_plot)

Within-pot percent change regression statistics

Since this data is a true within-pot pairing (not a crop average), the root vs. soil regression is meaningful here. This chunk fits soil_pct_change ~ root_pct_change for each region, (i) across all pots and (ii) grouped by crop. (There’s no “by timepoint” grouping this time, since wk10 -> wk40 is a single fixed period rather than multiple timepoints.)

# Generic helper: fit y_col ~ x_col, optionally within groups of group_var.
# Returns n, R-squared, slope, and p-value for each group (or one row if group_var is NULL).
calc_regression_stats <- function(data, x_col, y_col, group_var = NULL) {
  fit_one <- function(df, group_label) {
    df <- df[stats::complete.cases(df[, c(x_col, y_col)]), ]
    if (nrow(df) < 3 || stats::sd(df[[x_col]]) == 0) {
      return(data.frame(group = group_label, n = nrow(df),
                         r_squared = NA_real_, slope = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_))
    }
    model <- lm(reformulate(x_col, response = y_col), data = df)
    model_summary <- summary(model)
    data.frame(
      group = group_label,
      n = nrow(df),
      r_squared = model_summary$r.squared,
      slope = unname(coef(model)[2]),
      p_value = model_summary$coefficients[2, 4]
    )
  }

  if (is.null(group_var)) {
    return(fit_one(data, "All data"))
  }

  groups <- unique(data[[group_var]])
  if (group_var == "crop") {
    crop_order <- c("wheat", "rice", "soy", "noPlant")
    groups <- groups[order(match(groups, crop_order))]
  } else {
    groups <- sort(groups)
  }

  do.call(rbind, lapply(groups, function(g) fit_one(data[data[[group_var]] == g, ], as.character(g))))
}

region_labels <- c(simple_plant = "Simple Plant", complex_plant = "Complex Plant", microbial = "Microbial")

# Calculate stats for each region: (i) all pots, (ii) by crop
change_regression_stats_wk10_wk40 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(region_labels), function(region) {
  x_col <- paste0(region, "_root_pct_change")
  y_col <- paste0(region, "_soil_pct_change")

  stats_region <- rbind(
    calc_regression_stats(change_data_wk10_wk40, x_col, y_col, group_var = NULL),
    calc_regression_stats(change_data_wk10_wk40, x_col, y_col, group_var = "crop")
  )
  stats_region$region <- region_labels[[region]]
  stats_region
}))

# Tidy up for display
change_regression_stats_wk10_wk40 <- change_regression_stats_wk10_wk40 %>%
  select(region, group, n, r_squared, slope, p_value) %>%
  mutate(
    r_squared = round(r_squared, 3),
    slope = round(slope, 3),
    p_value = case_when(
      is.na(p_value) ~ NA_character_,
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "< 0.001",
      TRUE ~ format(round(p_value, 3), nsmall = 3)
    )
  )

knitr::kable(change_regression_stats_wk10_wk40,
             col.names = c("Region", "Group", "n", "R\u00b2", "Slope", "p-value"),
             row.names = FALSE)
Region Group n R² Slope p-value
Simple Plant All data 8 0.689 -0.952 0.011
Simple Plant wheat 2 NA NA NA
Simple Plant rice 3 0.807 -1.565 0.290
Simple Plant soy 3 0.419 -4.765 0.552
Complex Plant All data 8 0.626 -1.227 0.019
Complex Plant wheat 2 NA NA NA
Complex Plant rice 3 0.024 -0.995 0.900
Complex Plant soy 3 0.160 0.486 0.738
Microbial All data 8 0.743 -0.328 0.006
Microbial wheat 2 NA NA NA
Microbial rice 3 0.788 -0.670 0.304
Microbial soy 3 0.711 -0.640 0.361 
# Build the long-format dataset directly (no pivot_wider needed, avoids list-column
# issues if any crop+ID combination isn't perfectly unique going in)
change_data_long <- bind_rows(
  change_data_wk10_wk40 %>%
    transmute(crop, ID, region = "Simple Plant",
              root = simple_plant_root_pct_change,
              soil = simple_plant_soil_pct_change),
  change_data_wk10_wk40 %>%
    transmute(crop, ID, region = "Complex Plant",
              root = complex_plant_root_pct_change,
              soil = complex_plant_soil_pct_change),
  change_data_wk10_wk40 %>%
    transmute(crop, ID, region = "Microbial",
              root = microbial_root_pct_change,
              soil = microbial_soil_pct_change)
) %>%
  mutate(region = factor(region, levels = c("Simple Plant", "Complex Plant", "Microbial")))

soil_v_root_change_plot_combined_wk10_wk40 <- function(data) {
  min_val <- min(data$root, data$soil, na.rm = TRUE)
  max_val <- max(data$root, data$soil, na.rm = TRUE)
  range <- max_val - min_val
  lower <- min_val - 0.05 * range
  upper <- max_val + 0.05 * range
  step <- max(round(0.15 * range, 0), 1)
  breaks <- seq(
    from = step * floor(lower / step),
    to   = step * ceiling(upper / step),
    by   = step
  )

  region_shapes <- c("Simple Plant" = 16, "Complex Plant" = 17, "Microbial" = 4)
  region_strokes <- c("Simple Plant" = 0.5, "Complex Plant" = 0.5, "Microbial" = 1.5)

  plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = root, y = soil, color = crop, shape = region, stroke = region)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = annotation_line_color) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = annotation_line_color) +
    geom_point(size = 3.3, alpha = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = crop_colors, labels = crop_labels) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = region_shapes,
      guide = guide_legend(title = "Region", override.aes = list(color = annotation_text_color, size = 4))
      ) +
      scale_discrete_manual(
        aesthetics = "stroke",
        values = region_strokes,
        guide = guide_legend(title = "Region")
        ) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(lower, upper), breaks = breaks) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(lower, upper), breaks = breaks) +
    labs(title = "Change in OM Proportion (All Regions)",
         x = "% Change in Roots",
         y = "% Change in Rhizosphere Soil",
         color = "Crop"
         ) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 11.5, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      aspect.ratio = 1
    ) +
    geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = annotation_line_color)

  ggsave(
    file.path(outputs_folder, paste0("combined_regions_change_plot_wk10_wk40", theme_suffix, ".png")),
    plot, width = 7.6, height = 6.7
  )

  return(plot)
}

combined_change_plot_wk10_wk40 <- soil_v_root_change_plot_combined_wk10_wk40(change_data_long)
print_square_plot(combined_change_plot_wk10_wk40)
